name: Sweet Mini-Muffins
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Butter or margarine, at room temperature
1/4 cup Butter or margarine, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium-sized bowl, beat together the 1/4 cup butter and 1/2 cup sugar until blended.
2. Beat in the egg.
3. Sift together the flour, baking powder, salt, and nutmeg.
4. Stir the dry ingredients into the butter mixture alternately with milk, blending well after each addition.
5. Spoon the batter into greased 1 1/2-inch muffin cups, filling 2/3 to 3/4 full.
6. Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F for 12-18 minutes until golden brown.
7. Remove from pans while the muffins are still hot.
8. Dip each muffin into melted butter, coating evenly; then roll in the cinnamon-sugar mixture.
9. Place on a plate and let cool completely.
10. Wrap airtight and store at room temperature for up to 1 day, or in the freezer for up to 1 month (thaw frozen muffins unwrapped).
11. To reheat, arrange on a baking sheet, cover loosely with foil, and heat in a preheated oven at 350°F for about 10 minutes.
12. Makes 3 dozen mini-muffins.
